statement.latex: Let$G$be a group, and$(g_1, , g_n)$a tuple of its elements. The { type} of this tuple in$G$, denoted$Tp^G(g_1, , g_n)$, is the set of all first order formulas in free variables$x_1, ,x_n$in the standard group theory language which are true on$(g_1, , g_n)$in$G$. Two groups$G$and$H$are called isotypic if for every tuple of elements$h=(h_1, ,h_n)$in$H$there is a tuple$g =(g_1, ,g_n)$in$G$, such that...
